description: FORWARD DESIGN (spec) of agentNewSolution2 — Stage 1 of the split newModule flow. This agent lives in master frontend (102020) and is meant to be called by agentNewSolution. It delivers ONLY the durable business model (the 'behavior contract'): ontology + rules + workflows + operations. It STOPS before screens/BFF (Stage 2) and before backend persistence/implementation (Stage 3). Built on the learnings of analise10/11/12 and todo/newModuleFluxos.md (Workflow/Operation as the single generation + maintenance principle).

conventions.stageBoundary: Stage 1 delivers the BEHAVIOR MODEL only: ontology + rules + workflows + operations. It does NOT produce pages, per-page bffCommands, tables, persistence, layer_3/layer_4 backend, or metric tables. Those belong to Stage 2 (Experience) and Stage 3 (Backend), which are SEPARATE tasks depending only on the permanent artifacts produced here.
conventions.layerPlacement: l4 = BUSINESS. l5 = PROJECT DATA only (monitoring, project.json, infra). Therefore: ontology and the module structural map move to l4 (module-scoped); workflows, operations and rules live in l4 (global, outside the module, because they may span modules/horizontals).

artifactPaths.projectData: l5/project.json  (project dependencies / infra only — NOT business)

conventions.userStories: User stories / journeys are TRANSIENT input. They are absorbed into each Workflow and Operation as an embedded `story` block (actor, goal, soThat, steps, outcome) so the module is rich, not enxuto. NO journeys.defs.ts artifact is produced (fix vs mls-102048 where l5/{module}/journeys.defs.ts existed).
conventions.idSpace: All entity references (operation.reads/writes, workflow.entities, etc.) MUST use canonical ONTOLOGY entity ids, qualified with the module when cross-module (e.g. cafeFlow:Order). NEVER aggregate/group names (e.g. OrderAggregate, menuEntity) — root cause of the entity.ref.unknown drift in analise11/analise12.
conventions.ontologyIsDataOnly: ontology.entities contain ONLY persistent DATA nouns (kind core/mdm/event/metric/supporting). NEVER use-cases/workflows/operations/queries as ontology entities (no Uc*/verb-named entities) — blueprint guard from analise11.
conventions.rulesDestination: rules are saved to l4/rules — NEVER to trace/_traceTemp (bug observed in mls-102048).
conventions.criticLoop: Index steps (workflowIndex, operationIndex) keep the critic/repair loop, but with the analise11 hardening: deterministic errors stay hard; on critic non-convergence the index is accepted with remaining LLM findings downgraded to warnings (never hard-fail the task).
conventions.newTaskHandoff: This whole flow is ONE task. Stage 2 (Experience) and Stage 3 (Backend) start as NEW tasks that read ONLY the permanent l4 artifacts produced here. Maintenance re-runs this stage scoped to a single Workflow / Operation / Entity (blast-radius from dependsFiles / impact graph).
conventions.newAgents: agentClassifyBehavior, agentPlanOperationIndex, agentPlanOperationDefinition and agentValidateBehaviorModel are NEW (the Operation concept did not exist before). The other agentName values reuse existing planning agents from agentNewSolution.

treeOverview: org-domain (container; blueprint -> review -> finalize -> per-entity ontology + mdm/horizontals/plugins refs)
org-behavior (container; classify capabilities into Workflow|Operation, then define workflows and operations)
org-handoff (container; validate the behavior model, freeze artifacts, finish the task; optional resume screen)

steps.goal: Container for the requirements phase (clarification + discovery + recommendations + decisions).
First clarification with the user (small, no architecture).
Identify scope and signals. Platform-provided concerns (auth, audit, monitoring, notifications) are out of scope (analise11 T12).
Recommend artifacts at the BEHAVIOR level only: ontology entities, workflows, operations, MDM, horizontals, agents. Do NOT recommend pages or tables (later stages).
User accepts/defers recommendations.
Container (no LLM) for the domain phase (ontology + rules).

Generate the blueprint: module, actors, capabilities, ontology MAP, rules, relationships, and a behavior hint per capability (process vs direct action). GUARD: ontology.entities are data nouns only — never use-cases/workflows (analise11).
Detect domain/coverage gaps before specialized plans.
Apply the review and freeze the final plan (source of truth for the behavior plans).

output.result: FinalSolutionPlan { module, actors, capabilities, ontology MAP, rules, relationships, decisions }
savedArtifact: l4/{module}/module.defs.ts  (structural: actors, capabilities, ontology MAP — slim; canonical entity shapes live in l4/{module}/ontology/*)
l5/project.json  (project registration / deps only — merge, not overwrite)

steps.goal: Per-entity canonical shape (fields with per-field enum, statusEnum, lifecycle).
Plan MDM domains as references to the shared MDM infra (102034) when it exists.
Plan horizontal modules (reference if exists; draft otherwise). Notifications/audit/monitoring are platform-provided and NOT planned here (analise11 T12).
Plan external plugins (reuse existing by brand before drafting).
Container (no LLM) for the behavior phase (workflows + operations) — the heart of Stage 1.

NEW. Classify every priority-now capability/user-action into a Workflow (stateful, triggered, multi-actor over time) OR an Operation (direct action of one actor on one entity: create/update/query/view). Absorb the user stories here as the source for each owner's `story` (NOT persisted as journeys). Output the behavior index (workflowIds + operationIds, each with its story and the ontology entities it touches).
Index of workflows to detail (executionMode, trigger, actors, refs).

Per workflow: full definition — trigger, actors, states, transitions, the operationIds it orchestrates, ontology entities touched, rulesApplied, and the embedded `story` (absorbed user story). Entity refs use canonical ontology ids only.
NEW. Index of operations to detail — direct actions on entities not owned by a workflow step (CRUD + query/view, including dashboards as query/view operations).

NEW. Per operation: operationId, title, actor, entity (ontology), kind (create|update|delete|query|view), reads/writes (ontology entities/fields), rulesApplied, and the embedded `story`. This IS the intent-level BFF contract (no tables, no per-page bffCommands). Entity refs use canonical ontology ids only.
Container. Validate the behavior model and freeze it as the permanent handoff for Stage 2 (Experience) and Stage 3 (Backend).

NEW. Non-blocking coverage/integrity report for Stage 1: every priority-now capability is owned by exactly one Workflow or Operation; every entity ref resolves to a canonical ontology id (no aggregate names); rules referenced exist; every workflow's orchestrated operationIds exist. Deterministic; warnings do not block.

OPTIONAL summary screen. The task finishes once the behavior model is frozen (analise11 T17: finish early; viewing the resume is optional; traces are not cleared at finish). On finish it can open the next-stage tasks (Experience / Backend) that depend only on the frozen l4 artifacts.

handoffArtifacts.description: What Stage 1 freezes and what Stage 2/3 consume — nothing else.
produces: l4/operations/*.defs.ts (global operations = intent-level BFF contract, with embedded stories)
consumedBy: Stage 2 Experience (screens + concrete BFF wiring) — separate task, master frontend
Stage 3 Backend (persistence + implementation) — separate task, master backend

openDecisions: Operation schema: confirm final fields (proposal: operationId, title, actor, entity, kind, reads[], writes[], rulesApplied[], story{actor,goal,soThat,steps[],outcome}).
Cross-module entity id qualification format (e.g. cafeFlow:Order) for global workflows/operations referencing another module's ontology.
Whether agentNewSolution2 reuses the existing requirements/blueprint agents as-is or needs thin wrappers (here assumed reuse).
MDM/horizontal/plugin refs currently stay in l5/{id}/module.defs.ts + project.json deps (not moved to l4); confirm.
